nodes.label: Joseph Stalin
nodes.title: General Secretary and Bolshevik leader
nodes.bio: Georgian Bolshevik who rises from underground organiser and Civil War commissar to General Secretary. By the end of chapter 2 he stands at the center of an intimate Kremlin court and a strained marriage to Nadya.

nodes.label: Vyacheslav Molotov
nodes.title: Premier and Stalin's closest ally
nodes.bio: Disciplined, severe, and tireless, Molotov is the ally who works closest to Stalin. He and Polina live inside the Kremlin village and move in the same domestic circle as the Stalins.

nodes.label: Polina Molotova
nodes.title: Bolshevik organiser and Molotov's wife
nodes.bio: Bold and ambitious Bolshevik, admired by her husband and feared by some others. She is Nadya's closest friend and the woman who follows her out into the night after the quarrel.

nodes.label: Lazar Kaganovich
nodes.title: Stalin's deputy
nodes.bio: Boisterous, hard-driving deputy in Stalin's inner circle. He is one of the magnates who drops in and out of the Kremlin apartments like family.

nodes.label: Klim Voroshilov
nodes.title: Defence Commissar
nodes.bio: Genial Defence Commissar and old Tsaritsyn comrade. His apartment hosts the Revolution anniversary dinner that frames the prologue.

nodes.label: Ekaterina Voroshilova
nodes.title: Voroshilov's wife
nodes.bio: Part of the tight social world of Kremlin wives. Her diary remembers the leadership circle as simple, friendly, and unusually intimate.

nodes.label: Anastas Mikoyan
nodes.title: Senior Bolshevik organiser
nodes.bio: Shrewd Armenian Bolshevik who simply walks across the courtyard when he needs Stalin. He is one of the most sociable figures in the Kremlin family.

nodes.label: Leon Trotsky
nodes.title: War Commissar and chief rival
nodes.bio: Hero of the Civil War and creator of the Red Army. His struggle with Stalin over power and method shapes the political background of chapter 1.

nodes.label: Nadezhda Krupskaya
nodes.title: Lenin's wife and Bolshevik educator
nodes.bio: Long-time revolutionary and Lenin's companion in exile and power. Her role in Lenin's final political period is part of the succession context behind the early chapters.

nodes.label: Nikita Khrushchev
nodes.title: Young party organiser
nodes.bio: Energetic party worker from the Industrial Academy. Nadya notices him early and helps put his name before Stalin.

nodes.label: Alexander Poskrebyshev
nodes.title: Stalin's chief aide
nodes.bio: Stalin's dour chief aide, gatekeeper, and office manager. Nadya treats him as part of the family machine and writes to him with instructions from holiday.

nodes.label: Semyon Budyonny
nodes.title: Cavalry commander
nodes.bio: Swaggering cavalry commander from the Tsaritsyn years. He represents the martial, hard-drinking comradeship that helped carry Stalin upward.

nodes.label: Mikhail Kalinin
nodes.title: Head of state
nodes.bio: Ceremonial head of state and long-time visitor to the Alliluyev home. He belongs to the older generation of Bolshevik household regulars.

nodes.label: Yakov Djugashvili
nodes.title: Stalin's elder son
nodes.bio: Stalin's elder son from the first marriage. By the time of the prologue he is back in the Kremlin household after an unsettled youth.

nodes.label: Vasily Stalin
nodes.title: Stalin's son
nodes.bio: Young son of Stalin and Nadya, lively and difficult to discipline. He appears in the family letters and household scenes of the first two chapters.

nodes.label: Svetlana Stalin
nodes.title: Stalin's daughter
nodes.bio: Young daughter of Stalin and Nadya, present in the letters, routines, and domestic memories of the Kremlin home.

nodes.label: Anna Alliluyeva Redens
nodes.title: Nadya's sister
nodes.bio: Nadya's sister, present in family scenes from the Revolution to the last evening in the prologue.

nodes.label: Vladimir Lenin
nodes.title: Bolshevik leader
nodes.bio: Founder of the Bolshevik state and Stalin's political hero. He relies on Stalin in crisis, then grows alarmed by Stalin's accumulating power.

nodes.label: Artyom Sergeev
nodes.title: Adopted son in the Stalin household
nodes.bio: Adopted son who moves through the Stalin household as one of the children. He belongs to the everyday family world around Nadya.

nodes.label: Pavel Alliluyev
nodes.title: Red Army officer
nodes.bio: Nadya's brother, working for the Red Army and often returning with gifts from abroad. The dress and pistol tied to the prologue both come through him.

nodes.label: Sergei Alliluyev
nodes.title: Electrical worker and Bolshevik supporter
nodes.bio: Skilled electrician and veteran Bolshevik sympathiser whose home becomes a shelter for revolutionaries. His family forms one of Stalin's most important early personal networks.

nodes.label: Olga Alliluyeva (née Fedorenko)
nodes.title: Nadya's mother
nodes.bio: Strong-willed mother at the center of the Alliluyev household. Her home links family life, revolutionary politics, and the emotional world around Nadya.

nodes.label: Fyodor Alliluyev
nodes.title: Bolshevik aide and Nadya's brother
nodes.bio: Nadya's brother who questions Stalin in 1917, works in his Commissariat, and later goes with him to Tsaritsyn.

nodes.label: Carolina Til
nodes.title: Housekeeper in the Stalin apartment
nodes.bio: Housekeeper in Stalin and Nadya's Kremlin apartment. She discovers Nadya the next morning and raises the alarm.

nodes.label: Alexander "Alyosha" Svanidze
nodes.title: Banking official and Stalin's brother-in-law
nodes.bio: Kato's brother, an old Georgian Bolshevik from the same seminary world as Stalin. By chapter 2 he belongs to the extended Svanidze-Alliluyev family network that still gives Stalin a Georgian domestic refuge.

nodes.label: Maria Svanidze
nodes.title: Opera singer in Stalin's in-law circle
nodes.bio: Jewish Georgian opera singer married to Alyosha Svanidze. She belongs to the intimate in-law circle around Stalin and Nadya during the Kremlin-family years.

nodes.label: Stanislas Redens
nodes.title: Secret policeman and Anna's husband
nodes.bio: Anna Alliluyeva's husband and Nadya's brother-in-law. He links the Alliluyev family to the security world that increasingly surrounds Stalin's household.

nodes.label: Nikolai Vlasik
nodes.title: Bodyguard in Stalin's protective ring
nodes.bio: One of the guards shadowing Stalin's movements. In the prologue's aftermath his recollections help map the last hours before Nadya's death.

nodes.label: Yevgenia "Zhenya" Alliluyeva
nodes.title: Actress and Pavel's wife
nodes.bio: Pavel Alliluyev's glamorous wife, known as Zhenya. She arrives with Pavel in the immediate aftermath of Nadya's death and belongs to the close family circle.

nodes.label: Andrei Andreyev
nodes.title: Central Committee secretary and Kremlin magnate
nodes.bio: Rising party leader in the Kremlin household world. Stalin drops by the Andreyevs' apartment informally, showing how closely politics and domestic life overlap.

nodes.label: Dora Khazan
nodes.title: Deputy textiles official and Nadya's close friend
nodes.bio: Andreyev's wife and one of Nadya's closest companions. She studies with Nadya and shares the same tram rides, gossip, and domestic-political overlap of chapter 2.

nodes.label: Lavrentiy Beria
nodes.title: Transcaucasus GPU chief
nodes.bio: Cunning and ruthlessly ambitious Georgian who heads the GPU in Transcaucasia. He cultivates Stalin's favour during holidays on the Black Sea, positioning himself as an indispensable Georgian loyalist in the security apparatus.

nodes.label: Andrei Zhdanov
nodes.title: Gorky regional boss and Fourth Secretary
nodes.bio: Cultivated party boss promoted to Fourth Secretary after Kirov's murder. Stalin grooms him as an ideological enforcer and key lieutenant.

nodes.label: Nikolai Yezhov
nodes.title: Central Committee Secretary and rising NKVD figure
nodes.bio: Small and fanatically loyal, Yezhov rises rapidly through the party apparatus to oversee the investigation of Kirov's murder. He becomes the instrument of mass terror, lending his name to the Yezhovshchina.

nodes.label: Andrei Vyshinsky
nodes.title: Chief State Prosecutor
nodes.bio: Thundering prosecutor who stages the show trials with theatrical ferocity, browbeating defendants into public confessions. A former Menshevik who overcomes his suspect past through slavish loyalty to Stalin.

links.description: Closest political ally in the Kremlin leadership.
Deputy who helps enforce Stalin's line.
Old Tsaritsyn ally and host of the November dinner.
Trusted colleague who moves easily in and out of Stalin's apartment.
Civil War and succession rivalry defines one of Stalin's deepest grudges.
Revolutionary partnership at the center of Bolshevik political life in Lenin's final years.
Chief aide who manages Stalin's paperwork and access.
Civil War cavalry ally from Tsaritsyn.
Young son in the Kremlin household.
Young daughter whom Stalin openly dotes on.
Yakov is Stalin's son from the first marriage.
A devoted political marriage.
Long-time member of the ruling circle.
Hosts of one of the Kremlin's busiest households.
Comrades from the Civil War's cavalry world.
Lenin brings Stalin into the center of Bolshevik power.
Artyom moves through the household as an adopted son.
Parents at the center of the Alliluyev household.
Worked beside Stalin in 1917 and at Tsaritsyn.
The Alliluyev home is one of Stalin's early Bolshevik refuges.
Marriage connecting Nadya's sister to the security world.
Brother-in-law from Stalin's first Georgian marriage.
Marriage inside the Svanidze branch of Stalin's extended family.
In-law and familiar presence in the wider household circle.
Bodyguard shadowing Stalin through the Kremlin and dachas.
Marriage within Nadya's immediate family.
Another Kremlin magnate in Stalin's daily political and domestic orbit.
Prominent Kremlin couple living inside the same residential world as the Stalins.
Part of Nadya's closest circle among the Kremlin wives.
GPU chief in Transcaucasia reporting to and serving Stalin's security needs.
Stalin cultivates Beria as a useful Georgian enforcer and future security chief.
Fanatically devoted subordinate who carries out Stalin's most sensitive and violent assignments.
Loyal party secretary serving as Stalin's chosen enforcer of ideology and cultural discipline.
Stalin picks Zhdanov to fill the void left by Kirov, elevating him to the inner circle.
Serves as Stalin's chief instrument in the show trials, performing scripts written by the Kremlin.
Court prosecutor and secret police chief work in tandem to destroy the old Bolshevik guard.
